parsed_description: After analyzing the given sequence, the following protein domains or motifs are predicted: YXXL motif (endocytosis signal), Di-leucine internalization motif, and Transmembrane protein gp41 domain. The sequence appears to encode an HIV envelope glycoprotein (Env) precursor, containing both gp120 and gp41 subunits. The gp41 component acts as a class I viral fusion protein with heptad repeat regions that form coiled-coil structures (trimer-of-hairpins) to drive membrane fusion. The protein localizes to the host cell endosome membrane, host cell plasma membrane, plasma membrane, viral envelope, and virion membrane. Functional activities include virion attachment to host cell, fusion of virus membrane with host endosome membrane and plasma membrane, clathrin-dependent endocytosis of virus by host cell, mitigation of host antiviral defense response, positive regulation of receptor clustering, positive regulation of establishment of T cell polarity, positive regulation of plasma membrane raft polarization, actin filament reorganization, and viral protein processing.

parsed_description: Component of the chromosomal passenger complex (CPC), a complex that acts as a key regulator of mitosis. The CPC complex has essential functions at the centromere in ensuring correct chromosome alignment and segregation and is required for chromatin-induced microtubule stabilization and spindle assembly. Acts as a scaffold regulating CPC localization and activity. The C-terminus associates with AURKB or AURKC, the N-terminus associated with BIRC5/survivin and CDCA8/borealin tethers the CPC to the inner centromere, and the microtubule binding activity within the central SAH domain directs AURKB/C toward substrates near microtubules. The flexibility of the SAH domain is proposed to allow AURKB/C to follow substrates on dynamic microtubules while ensuring CPC docking to static chromatin. Activates AURKB and AURKC. Required for localization of CBX5 to mitotic centromeres. Controls the kinetochore localization of BUB1.
